channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Smallest worthwhile effect of anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction compared with non-surgical management: a benefit-harm trade-off study.
item.description: A benefit-harm trade-off study via an online survey involving 92 recreationally or competitively active individuals estimated the smallest worthwhile effect of ACL reconstruction versus non-surgical management on knee function and return to sport, and explored associations with participant characteristics.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [commentary].
item.description: This critically appraised paper discusses that mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than center-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease patients.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Psychometric Properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale Used After Stroke: A Systematic Review Using COSMIN Methodology.
item.description: A systematic review using COSMIN methodology evaluated the psychometric properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale (TIS) in individuals with stroke, finding high certainty evidence for its structural validity, internal consistency, and reliability.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cost-Effectiveness of Post-Acute Stroke Rehabilitation From a U.S. Healthcare Payer Perspective: Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities Versus Skilled Nursing Facilities.
item.description: This decision analytic model evaluated the cost-effectiveness of Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities (IRF) versus Skilled Nursing Facilities (SNF) for post-acute stroke rehabilitation from a U.S. healthcare payer perspective.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Patterns of Outcome Measure Utilization in Stroke Rehabilitation Lower Extremity Trials Conducted in Low-to-Middle versus High-Income Countries.
item.description: This review of 1,548 RCTs on post-stroke lower extremity rehabilitation found that low-to-middle-income countries (LMICs) more frequently use generalized impairment and activities of daily living outcome measures relying on patient observation compared to high-income countries (HICs).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Anti-Compensatory Saccades Changes After Visuo-Vestibular Physical Therapy in People With Acute Unilateral Vestibulopathy: A Prospective Observational Study.
item.description: A prospective observational study of 92 AUVP patients found significant changes in anti-compensatory saccades (AcS) after an 8-week visuo-vestibular physical therapy (VVPT) program, suggesting AcS as a potential marker for central compensation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prioritising fall prevention strategies in Parkinson's disease: A multi-method evaluation.
item.description: A multi-method study integrating a narrative review, a two-round Delphi process with 15 experts, and multi-criteria decision-making identified and quantitatively prioritized fall-prevention strategies for people with Parkinson's disease.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in patients with stroke.
item.description: This prospective observational study examined 4-week inpatient rehabilitation outcomes in post-stroke hemiplegia patients, finding associations between neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with poorer functional status, quality of life, and depressive symptoms.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 17 trials (1,372 participants) found that shortwave diathermy (SWD) significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [-1.08, -0.37]; p < .01) in knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: Spine. Exploratory retrospective cohort. To determine the impact of sarcopenia on adjacent segment degeneration (ASD) following anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF). Sarcopenia has been show
item.author: PubMed

item.title: [Correspondence] Make rehabilitation count: invest in people, not just devices
item.description: This correspondence highlights the need to invest in people rather than solely devices for rehabilitation, as WHO's global rehabilitation indicators shift focus from disease to functioning and quality of life.
item.author: The Lancet

item.title: Personalized blood flow restriction training at variable occlusion pressures improves multisystem function in overweight and obese older women - European Journal of Applied Physiology
item.description: This research demonstrated that personalized blood flow restriction training at variable occlusion pressures improves multisystem function in overweight and obese older women.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Eight weeks of visual perturbation training effects on unanticipated cutting lower-limb biomechanics and errors in male athletes following anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction: a randomized controlled clinical trial (RCT).
item.description: An RCT with 24 ACLR male athletes who had returned to sport showed that 8 weeks of visual perturbation training significantly improved unanticipated cutting lower-limb biomechanics and reduced errors compared to control.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Preoperative physiotherapy and one-year patient-reported outcomes after primary total knee arthroplasty: a registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients.
item.description: A registry-based cohort study of 1,688 patients found no significant difference in KOOS-PS and pain at 1 year post-primary total knee arthroplasty between those who received preoperative physiotherapy and those who did not.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Short-term effects of therapeutic massage alone or combined with conventional physiotherapy in myofascial pain syndrome: A randomized controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial with 60 participants found that therapeutic massage, conventional physiotherapy, and combined treatment all improved pain, pressure pain threshold, and functional disability in individuals with chronic upper trapezius myofascial pain syndrome over the short term.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The WalkBack (walking and education) program for prevention of low back pain is acceptable to physiotherapists but relies on training, flexibility and system-level enablers to optimise implementation: a qualitative study.
item.description: A qualitative study of 20 Australian physiotherapists found that the WalkBack program for low back pain prevention is acceptable, but its implementation in routine practice relies on training, flexibility, and system-level enablers.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Predictive socio-cultural factors of pain intensity, disability, and quality of life in patients with nonspecific musculoskeletal pain: a prospective cohort study.
item.description: A prospective cohort study of 1,350 patients with non-specific musculoskeletal pain found that socio-cultural factors were associated with and predicted pain intensity, disability, and health-related quality of life at baseline, 3, 6, and 12 months.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Decision support in the management of musculoskeletal disorders: a qualitative study of physiotherapists' and patients' experiences.
item.description: A qualitative study explored the experiences of 11 patients and 10 physiotherapists in Norwegian primary healthcare with a CDSS (SupportPrim PT) for musculoskeletal disorders, highlighting its influence on decision-making processes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Stroke-specific Movement Patterns During Passage Through a Narrow-Opening: A Cross-Sectional Study.
item.description: NeuroRehabilitation. PurposeIndividuals with stroke have difficulty rotating their trunk and adjusting their supporting foot when negotiating spatial constraints. This study aimed to classify movement
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Self-management in neurological disorders: a scoping review of behavior change theories, strategies, contents, and impact.
item.description: Disability and rehabilitation. This scoping review aimed to identify and map the behavior change theories, behavior change techniques (BCTs), and effectiveness of self-management (SM) interventions de
item.author: PubMed
